headline: Calgary Market Softens in March as Inventory Builds
summary: Calgary's housing market showed signs of cooling in March 2026, with sales declining 12.9% year-over-year and the overall benchmark price slipping to $565,600 — down 4.2% from March 2025. Active listings climbed 5.1% year-over-year as more supply entered the market, giving buyers improved choice across all property types. Conditions remain broadly balanced with a sales-to-new-listings ratio of 55%.
insights: Detached home sales fell year-over-year but the segment's benchmark of $741,300 held up better than most, declining only 3.3% — a sign of continued demand for ground-oriented housing.
Apartment and townhouse prices saw the steepest year-over-year declines (-9.3% and -6.2%), creating improved entry-level opportunities for first-time and downsizing buyers.
At 35 days on market — up from 29 in March 2025 — sellers are taking longer to find buyers, and well-priced homes in desirable neighbourhoods are standing out more than ever.
A 55% sales-to-new-listings ratio confirms balanced conditions: neither buyers nor sellers hold a strong hand, making realistic pricing and skilled negotiation critical.
